codeReview.summary: The mission-plan command update satisfies the planning requirement: it now explicitly instructs the model to use activate_skill for mission-planner, references both the mission directory and the working directory from state.json, and preserves the planning-state guard before activation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker procedure expected the extension-builder skill to be invokable through the Skill tool, but it was only available as a file under .factory/skills and not registered in the skill system. This caused the worker to deviate from the prescribed procedure and read the skill file directly instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl session 04ef0779-3967-46b9-9581-51255d75384e shows `Skill {"skill": "extension-builder"}` returning `Error: Skill "extension-builder" not found`, followed by reading /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/.factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md; handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges records the same gap.

summary: Reviewed feature mission-plan-command against its metadata, worker handoff/transcript, and commit d899db3. The command passes review: it implements the required activate_skill wording and mission/work directory references without introducing code issues. One shared-state gap was noted: the extension-builder skill referenced by worker procedure is not registered for Skill-tool invocation.
